tiers.name: Not Ready
tiers.description: High risk. Start with governance, data, and workflow clarity.

tiers.name: Foundation Ready
tiers.description: Some foundation exists. Prioritize low-risk administrative use cases.

tiers.name: Pilot Ready
tiers.description: Ready for structured pilots with monitoring and human oversight.

tiers.name: Scale Ready
tiers.description: Ready to expand validated use cases across departments.

tiers.name: Enterprise AI Mature
tiers.description: Strong governance, integration, workforce, and monitoring capabilities.

categories.name: Strategy
questions: Does the organization have a documented AI strategy?
Is there executive sponsorship for AI initiatives?
Are AI use cases tied to measurable business or clinical outcomes?

categories.name: Data Readiness
questions: Is core clinical and operational data accessible and reliable?
Does the organization have data governance standards?
Can data be integrated across EHR, claims, financial, and operational systems?

categories.name: Technology Readiness
questions: Does the organization have modern integration capabilities?
Are APIs, cloud, security, and identity controls mature enough for AI?
Can AI tools be embedded into existing workflows?

categories.name: Workforce Readiness
questions: Do clinicians and staff understand approved AI use?
Is there an AI literacy or training plan?
Are workflows designed around human-in-the-loop validation?

categories.name: Governance & Compliance
questions: Is there an AI governance committee or review board?
Are AI tools evaluated for accuracy, bias, privacy, and safety?
Is there a shadow-AI policy?

categories.name: Use Case Prioritization
questions: Are use cases prioritized by ROI, risk, workflow fit, and complexity?
Are high-burden / low-risk workflows identified?
Are pilot success metrics defined before purchase?

categories.name: Vendor Readiness
questions: Does the organization have a vendor evaluation process for AI?
Are vendors reviewed for evidence, references, security, and interoperability?
Are referral or commercial conflicts disclosed?

categories.name: ROI Readiness
questions: Can the organization measure baseline cost, time, quality, and access metrics?
Are savings assumptions tied to real operational data?
Is there an owner accountable for realizing benefits?

categories.name: Implementation Capacity
questions: Are PM, change management, analysts, integration, and clinical champions available?
Is there a plan for training, rollout, and support?
Is post-go-live optimization resourced?

categories.name: Risk & Monitoring
questions: Are post-deployment monitoring processes defined?
Are escalation paths defined for AI errors or safety issues?
Are model performance and user behavior reviewed regularly?
